steps: To purify mCherry-WIPI2d and mCherry-WIPI3, as described previously for WIPI2d (Fracchiolla et al. 2020, J Cell Biol, PMID: 32437499), fuse the coding sequence of WIPI2d or WIPI3 to a N-terminal 6xHis-TEV-mCherry-tag through cloning into a pET-DUET1 vector (available from Addgene).

After the transformation of the pET-DUET1 vector encoding 6xHis-TEV-mCherry-WIPI2d/WIPI3 in E. coli Rosetta pLysS cells (Novagen Cat# 70956-4), grow the cells in 2x Tryptone Yeast extract (TY) medium at 37 °C until an OD 600 of 0.4 and then continue at 18 °C .
Once the cells reach an OD 600 of 0.8, induce the protein expression with 100 micromolar (µM) isopropyl β-D-1-thiogalactopyranoside (IPTG) for 16:00:00 at 18 °C .
Filter the supernatant through an 0.45 µm filter and load onto a pre-equilibrated 5 ml His-Trap HP column (Cytiva).
Clear the lysates by centrifugation at 18000 rpm, 4 °C, 45 minutes in a SORVAL RC6+ centrifuge with an F21S-8x50Y rotor (Thermo Scientific).
Collect the cells by centrifugation and resuspend in lysis buffer, complete EDTA-free protease inhibitors (Roche), CIP protease inhibitor (Sigma), and DNase (Sigma)). Lysis buffer: %s %s
Pool the fractions containing purified mCherry-WIPI2d or mCherry-WIPI3.
Analyse the fractions by SDS-PAGE and Coomassie staining.
Elute the proteins with SEC buffer. SEC buffer: %s %s

Pool the fractions containing the 6xHis-TEV-mCherry-WIPI2d/WIPI3, concentrate using a 30 kDa cut-off Amicon filter (Merck Millipore) and loade onto a pre-equilibrated Superdex 200 Increase 10/300 GL column (Cytiva).
Elute the proteins with a stepwise imidazole gradient (30, 75, 100, 150, 225, 300 mM).
After bind His tagged proteins to the column, wash the column with three column volumes of wash buffer. Wash buffer: %s %s
After concentrating the purified protein, aliquot the protein and snap-frozen in liquid nitrogen.
creator: Elias Adriaenssens
